codeReview.summary: The fix commit correctly makes two CLI active-routing tests availability-aware, but as a fix review it does not address the prior failed feature's blocking findings, which were in cross-surface contract coverage and not in this file.

issues.description: Scope mismatch with the prior failure: this fix only updates CLI active-routing availability assertions, while the prior failed review (.factory/validation/integration-hardening/scrutiny/reviews/integration-hardening-verify-cli-launch-chain-test-discovery.json) flagged unresolved blocking gaps in cross-surface contract tests. Neither reviewed commit modifies AgentLensTests/Active/SwitcherCrossFlowTests.swift, so the original failure is not remediated by this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked followedProcedure=true but did not provide required context-read evidence in verification.commandsRun.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-04-10T04-11-11-081Z...f64d9b4f...json has skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true and only test/script commands under verification.commandsRun; switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires explicit context-read evidence for followedProcedure=true ("Context Read Evidence for followedProcedure=true").

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS tracks one environment-dependent browser negative-path test but not the analogous CLI availability-dependent rapid-switch tests that also need availability-aware assertions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md documents SwitcherBrowserLaunchTests environment sensitivity under Known Pre-Existing Issues, while this feature/handoff had to harden CLI active-routing tests for executable availability.

summary: Reviewed fix feature f64d9b4f and both relevant diffs (9039c7a..., ddbcf9d...) plus transcript skeleton and prior review. Although 9039c7a makes rapid-switch CLI tests environment-agnostic as described, it does not resolve the original failed feature's blocking cross-surface gaps, so this fix review is failed.
